interpretation: The proposed $1,500/ac floor sits within US bounds: above the capitalised-rent value ($304 US average, $760 in Corn Belt at r=5%) and below the pasture-land-value average ($1,830 US average, $3,540 in Corn Belt). The sensitivity grid ($1,000-$2,000) brackets both ends. The Csikós-Tóth (2023) Hungarian citation is replaced by USDA-NASS Land Values 2024.
